Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add peek command to view session output

shuttle peek <n> [lines] - show last N lines from a session
without boarding. Defaults to 20 lines. Useful for quick
visibility into what an agent is doing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(SKEIN_AGENT_ID=prime skein post brief shuttle --title "Resume sessions from search results" "$(cat <<''EOF''
## Context
`shuttle search` finds past sessions by content. Natural next step: resume that session.

## Proposed Solution
`shuttle resume <session-id>` - resume a CC session by its UUID

CC has `--resume` flag. Need to:
1. Map session UUID → resumable session
2. Launch CC with `--resume` in new tmux session
3. Possibly need to specify project directory too

## Questions
- Does CC''s `--resume` work with arbitrarily old sessions?
- Is the session ID sufficient or do we need more context?
- Should this create a new tmux session or attach to existing?

## Use Cases
- "I was working on auth yesterday, let me pick up where I left off"
- Search → find relevant session → resume instead of starting fresh

## Acceptance
- Can resume a session found via `shuttle search`
- Conversation context is preserved
EOF
)")

Bash(SKEIN_AGENT_ID=prime skein post brief shuttle --title "Export session as markdown" "$(cat <<''EOF''
## Context
Sometimes you want to share a conversation, archive it, or mine it for patterns. Currently need to manually copy or read raw JSONL.

## Proposed Solution
`shuttle export <n|session-id> [--file out.md]`

Output format:
```markdown
# Session: {session-id}
Project: {project}
Date: {timestamp}

## User
{message}

## Claude
{response}

## User
...
```

Options:
- `--json` - output as cleaned JSON instead of markdown
- `--file` - write to file instead of stdout
- `--last N` - only last N messages

## Use Cases
- Share interesting conversations
- Archive sessions for later reference
- Feed to other tools for analysis
- Build a personal "agentology" corpus

## Acceptance
- Readable markdown output
- Includes metadata (project, date, session ID)
- Can export by tmux session number or raw session UUID
EOF
)")

Bash(SKEIN_AGENT_ID=prime skein post brief shuttle --title "Robust CC session index for agentology" "$(cat <<''EOF''
## Context
CC stores sessions in `~/.claude/projects/{path}/*.jsonl`. Currently `shuttle search` does live grep which is slow for large history. A proper index would enable:
- Fast search across all sessions
- Metadata queries (by date, project, duration)
- Foundation for semantic search later

## Proposed Solution
Build and maintain a session index:

### Index Storage
`~/.shuttle/index.json` or SQLite:
```json
{
  "sessions": {
    "uuid-1": {
      "project": "/home/patrick/projects/foo",
      "started": "2025-12-15T10:00:00Z",
      "last_activity": "2025-12-15T12:00:00Z",
      "message_count": 45,
      "summary": "Working on auth system...",
      "tags": ["auth", "backend"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Commands
- `shuttle index` - rebuild full index
- `shuttle index --update` - incremental update (new/modified files)
- Search uses index instead of live grep

### Future: Semantic Search
With index in place, could add:
- Embeddings for each session (summary or full text)
- Vector similarity search
- "Find sessions like this one"

## Use Cases
- Fast search across months of sessions
- "What was I working on last Tuesday?"
- Mining patterns across agent conversations
- Building agentology corpus

## Acceptance
- Index builds in reasonable time
- Search is noticeably faster
- Index updates incrementally
EOF
)")
